title: erigeron uniflorus subsp. uniflorus
intro: Erigeron uniflorus subsp. uniflorus, also known as the western daisy or alpine fleabane, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family that typically grows in high-altitude mountainous regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Erigeron uniflorus subsp. uniflorus include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, fever, and sore throats.
